sections.paragraph:
m.-

(1) subst. perpetuity, continuance (so called from proceeding, going on, root מוּד, compare עַד from the root עָדָה, דֹור and תְּדִירָא from דּוּר). In genit. put after other substantives it is used as an adjective (compare קֹדֶשׁ No. 1), as אַנְשֵׁי תָמִיד “men of continuance,” i.e. hired for continuous work, Eze 39:14 עֹולַת הַתָּמִיד a continual burnt-offering, i.e. daily, both morning and evening, Num 28:6, 10 Num 28:10, 15 Num 28:15, 23 Num 28:23, 24 Num 28:24. לֶחֶם הַתָּמִיד the continual bread, i.q. לֶחֶם פָּנִים Num 4:7.

(2) for עֹולַת הַתָּמִיד Dan 8:11, 12 , 13 11:31 .

(3) adv. continually, Psa 16:8 25:15 34:2 .
